run_summary: Screen 1 faces a portfolio management inflection: CGNX hits day 15 (exit discipline triggered), while ONTO/AEIS/PLAB show fresh OVERDONE flags on the same OpenAI-capex-cutback catalyst — applying the basket rule. DOCN flags on a separate Jalapeno catalyst.

entries.name: DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI unveils its first custom chip, built by Broadcom
entries.thesis: OpenAI building its own inference chip is a vertical-integration story aimed at hyperscale AI inference economics — it has essentially zero direct read-through to DOCN's developer/SMB cloud business. DOCN is being lumped into 'cloud infrastructure providers at risk' by the trigger taxonomy, but its customer base and workload mix don't overlap with what Jalapeno disrupts. The sharp single-day drop on an already-extended week down looks like thematic AI-infra de-risking rather than a justified threat reaction.
entries.what_kills: Disclosure that a meaningful chunk of DOCN's AI/ML compute revenue comes from labs that might in-source (low probability given customer profile); guidance cut citing AI-infra competitive pricing; continued decline suggesting fundamental rather than sympathy selling.
entries.reasoning: DOCN flags OVERDONE on OpenAI Jalapeno custom-chip catalyst (threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'). Filings evidence is solid: DOCN's developer/SMB customer base does not overlap with hyperscale in-sourcing dynamics; AI/ML is a disclosed growth vector but customer profile is indie devs, not OpenAI-scale labs. Confidence=3 (exactly at threshold); threat_assessment='minimal' (not 'none'). Meets exploratory-tier bar. Not a basket rule violation (separate catalyst from AEIS/PLAB/ONTO). 5-day move is -14.69% — extended but consistent with 1-day panic (-5.47%) on a thematic trigger. Size at 6% exploratory.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case assumes the panic is purely thematic sympathy, but the 5-day decline of -14.69% combined with volume only 1.06x average suggests weak conviction buying interest, leaving the door open for further deterioration if sentiment stays negative.
red_team.critique: The bull thesis rests on customer-base segmentation (indie devs ≠ hyperscale labs) being self-evident and immediately re-priced. But the five-day move of -14.69% is extended, and volume_multiple of only 1.06x suggests the selloff lacked panic-flush characteristics—instead it looks like steady unwind without a corresponding bounce attempt or stabilization signal. If this were pure thematic panic, you'd expect either a spike to 2.0x+ volume on the initial shock, or a quick half-life fade; instead, DOCN appears to be leaking lower on ordinary volume, which is consistent with fundamental re-rating (investor confidence in cloud SMB growth under threat) rather than sympathy flush. The bull case names no concrete near-term catalyst to arrest the decline beyond 'the news cycle moves on,' which is passive rather than active conviction.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
entries.catalyst: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request, says restrictions shouldn't be the norm
entries.thesis: AEIS sells precision power conversion for semiconductor fabs and data center computing. An OpenAI model release — especially one with restricted rollout — does nothing to reduce demand for the power-delivery infrastructure underneath AI training/inference. The selloff is mechanically backwards: more AI capability deployment = more data center power = more AEIS content.
entries.what_kills: An actual hyperscaler capex cut announcement, a specific AEIS guide-down on data center segment, or a customer concentration disclosure showing dependence on a single OpenAI-adjacent buyer.
entries.reasoning: AEIS is already held (days_held=2, unrealized -5.02%). Screen 1 does not support ADD; the sympathy-fade thesis has a fixed 5-15 day window and averaging into an underwater position violates discipline. Thesis is intact (fresh flag confirms), so HOLD the existing position — do not open a second tranche.

entries.name: Photronics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request, says restrictions shouldn't be the norm
entries.thesis: Photronics makes photomasks — the templates used to manufacture every chip, including every AI accelerator and HBM die. An OpenAI model release with restricted rollout has zero substitutive relationship to mask demand; if anything, more advanced AI models require more advanced nodes, which require more complex (and more expensive) masks. Pure semi-sympathy sell.
entries.what_kills: A specific customer order cancellation, a step-down in advanced-node mask pricing, or evidence that AI accelerator designs are consolidating around fewer mask sets.
entries.reasoning: PLAB is already held (days_held=1). Fresh flag reinforces thesis but Screen 1 does not support ADD. HOLD existing position; do not double up.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request, says restrictions shouldn't be the norm
entries.thesis: Onto sells process control (inspection, metrology, lithography) for advanced semiconductor manufacturing including HBM and advanced packaging — both of which are critical to AI accelerator production. A model release shipped with constrained access has no bearing on metrology tool demand. AI chip complexity is increasing process control intensity per wafer, which is structurally bullish for Onto.
entries.what_kills: A foundry-specific capex cut affecting advanced packaging, customer concentration disclosure revealing high single-customer AI exposure that gets reduced, or guide-down tied to specific AI accelerator program.
entries.reasoning: ONTO is already held (days_held=2, +0.87% unrealized). Fresh flag validates sympathy-fade thesis but Screen 1 prohibits ADD on existing positions. The duplicate day-0 entry should be exited (see position decision above). Do not open a third tranche.
